remote@npsM1:~$ cat /home/remote/homeassistant/custom_components/snapmaker_u1/__init__.py
import os
import asyncio
import logging
import re
import json
import urllib.parse
import aiohttp
from datetime import timedelta
from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntry
from homeassistant.core import HomeAssistant
from homeassistant.helpers.update_coordinator import DataUpdateCoordinator, UpdateFailed
from homeassistant.helpers.aiohttp_client import async_get_clientsession
from homeassistant.components.http import StaticPathConfig, HomeAssistantView
from .const import DOMAIN, CONF_IP_ADDRESS, POLL_INTERVAL

_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)

PLATFORMS = ["sensor", "number", "switch", "button", "camera", "text"]

class SnapmakerUploadView(HomeAssistantView):
    """Secure Proxy to upload files from the browser through HA to the printer."""
    url = "/api/snapmaker_u1/upload"
    name = "api:snapmaker_u1:upload"
    requires_auth = True

    async def post(self, request):
        hass = request.app["hass"]
        session = async_get_clientsession(hass)
        try:
            reader = await request.multipart()
            ip_address = None
            file_content = None
            filename = "upload.gcode"
            t0_map = 0

            while True:
                part = await reader.next()
                if part is None:
                    break
                if part.name == "ip":
                    ip_address = await part.text()
                elif part.name == "t0_map":
                    t0_map = int(await part.text())
                elif part.name == "file":
                    filename = part.filename
                    file_content = await part.read()

            if not ip_address or not file_content:
                return self.json({"error": "Missing IP or File"}, status_code=400)

            # THE MAGIC: Apply T0 tool mapping regex if needed
            if t0_map != 0:
                try:
                    text_content = file_content.decode('utf-8')
                    text_content = re.sub(r'\bT0\b', f'T{t0_map}', text_content)
                    file_content = text_content.encode('utf-8')
                except UnicodeDecodeError:
                    pass # Ignore if binary G-Code

            form = aiohttp.FormData()
            form.add_field('print', 'false')
            form.add_field('file', file_content, filename=filename)

            async with session.post(f"http://{ip_address}:7125/server/files/upload", data=form) as resp:
                result = await resp.json()
                return self.json(result)

        except Exception as e:
            _LOGGER.error(f"Snapmaker Upload Proxy Error: {e}")
            return self.json({"error": str(e)}, status_code=500)

async def async_setup_entry(hass: HomeAssistant, entry: ConfigEntry) -> bool:
    ip_address = entry.data[CONF_IP_ADDRESS]
    session = async_get_clientsession(hass)

    card_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "snapmaker-card.js")
    await hass.http.async_register_static_paths([
        StaticPathConfig("/snapmaker_u1/snapmaker-card.js", card_path, False)
    ])

    # Register the Upload Proxy
    hass.http.register_view(SnapmakerUploadView())

    sw_version = "Unknown"
    try:
        async with session.get(f"http://{ip_address}:7125/printer/info", timeout=5) as info_res:
            info_json = await info_res.json()
            sw_version = info_json.get("result", {}).get("software_version", "Moonraker")
    except Exception:
        pass

    async def async_update_data():
        try:
            url = f"http://{ip_address}:7125/printer/objects/query?webhooks&print_stats&toolhead&extruder&extruder1&extruder2&extruder3&heater_bed&fan&gcode_move&display_status&virtual_sdcard&print_task_config"
            async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as response:
                response.raise_for_status()
                data = await response.json()
                status = data.get("result", {}).get("status", {})

            try:
                files_url = f"http://{ip_address}:7125/server/files/list?root=gcodes"
                async with session.get(files_url, timeout=5) as files_res:
                    files_json = await files_res.json()
                    status["gcode_files"] = files_json.get("result", [])
            except Exception:
                status["gcode_files"] = []

            return status
        except Exception as err:
            raise UpdateFailed(f"Error communicating with API: {err}")

    coordinator = DataUpdateCoordinator(
        hass, _LOGGER, name="snapmaker_u1_coordinator",
        update_method=async_update_data, update_interval=timedelta(seconds=POLL_INTERVAL)
    )

    coordinator.snapmaker_sw_version = sw_version
    coordinator.snapmaker_serial = ip_address.replace(".", "")

    await coordinator.async_config_entry_first_refresh()
    hass.data.setdefault(DOMAIN, {})[entry.entry_id] = coordinator

    # --- NEW HA SERVICES FOR LAUNCHING AND DELETING ---
    async def handle_start_job(call):
        ip = call.data.get("ip_address")
        filename = call.data.get("filename")
        bed = call.data.get("bed_leveling", True)
        timelapse = call.data.get("timelapse", False)
        multicolor = call.data.get("multicolor", False)
        t0 = int(call.data.get("t0_map", 0))
        t1 = int(call.data.get("t1_map", 1))
        t2 = int(call.data.get("t2_map", 2))
        t3 = int(call.data.get("t3_map", 3))
        is_upload_flow = call.data.get("is_upload_flow", False)

        mapping = [t0, t1, t2, t3] + [0]*28 if multicolor else [t0, 1, 2, 3] + [0]*28
        base_url = f"http://{ip}:7125"

        bed_str = "True" if bed else "False"
        tl_str = "True" if timelapse else "False"
        map_str = "[" + ",".join(str(x) for x in mapping) + "]"

        # 1. Update Klipper Variables
        await session.post(f"{base_url}/printer/gcode/script?script=SET_GCODE_VARIABLE MACRO=print_task_config VARIABLE=auto_bed_leveling VALUE={bed_str}")
        await session.post(f"{base_url}/printer/gcode/script?script=SET_GCODE_VARIABLE MACRO=print_task_config VARIABLE=time_lapse_camera VALUE={tl_str}")
        await session.post(f"{base_url}/printer/gcode/script?script=SET_GCODE_VARIABLE MACRO=print_task_config VARIABLE=extruder_map_table VALUE=\"{map_str}\"")

        # 2. Upload print_task.json to keep machine UI synced
        task_data = {
            "auto_bed_leveling": bed, "time_lapse_camera": timelapse, "flow_calibrate": False,
            "extruder_map_table": mapping, "extruders_used": [True, True, True, True]
        }
        form = aiohttp.FormData()
        form.add_field('root', 'config')
        form.add_field('file', json.dumps(task_data), filename='print_task.json', content_type='application/json')
        await session.post(f"{base_url}/server/files/upload", data=form)

        # 3. Apply Regex to existing files triggered from Internal Storage
        if not is_upload_flow and t0 != 0:
            async with session.get(f"{base_url}/server/files/gcodes/{urllib.parse.quote(filename)}") as resp:
                if resp.status == 200:
                    content = await resp.text()
                    content = re.sub(r'\bT0\b', f'T{t0}', content)
                    uform = aiohttp.FormData()
                    uform.add_field('print', 'false')
                    uform.add_field('file', content.encode('utf-8'), filename=filename, content_type='text/plain')
                    await session.post(f"{base_url}/server/files/upload", data=uform)

        # 4. Start the print
        await asyncio.sleep(0.5)
        await session.post(f"{base_url}/printer/print/start?filename={urllib.parse.quote(filename)}")
        await coordinator.async_request_refresh()

    async def handle_delete_file(call):
        ip = call.data.get("ip_address")
        filename = call.data.get("filename")
        await session.delete(f"http://{ip}:7125/server/files/gcodes/{urllib.parse.quote(filename)}")
        await coordinator.async_request_refresh()

    # 🌟 NEW: SECURE SPOOL EDITOR
    async def handle_edit_spool(call):
        ip = call.data.get("ip_address")
        tool = int(call.data.get("tool", 0))
        material = call.data.get("material", "PLA")
        color_hex = call.data.get("color", "FFFFFF").replace("#", "")

        try:
            color_dec = int(color_hex, 16)
            payload = {
                "channel": tool,
                "info": {
                    "VENDOR": "Generic",
                    "MAIN_TYPE": material,
                    "RGB_1": color_dec,
                    "ALPHA": 255
                }
            }
            async with session.post(f"http://{ip}:7125/printer/filament_detect/set", json=payload) as resp:
                await resp.read()
            await asyncio.sleep(1)
            await coordinator.async_request_refresh()
        except Exception as e:
            _LOGGER.error(f"Failed to edit spool: {e}")

    hass.services.async_register(DOMAIN, "start_print_job", handle_start_job)
    hass.services.async_register(DOMAIN, "delete_file", handle_delete_file)
    hass.services.async_register(DOMAIN, "edit_spool", handle_edit_spool)

    await hass.config_entries.async_forward_entry_setups(entry, PLATFORMS)
    return True

async def async_unload_entry(hass: HomeAssistant, entry: ConfigEntry) -> bool:
    unload_ok = await hass.config_entries.async_unload_platforms(entry, PLATFORMS)
    if unload_ok:
        hass.data[DOMAIN].pop(entry.entry_id)
    return unload_ok
